earnings.source: Broadcom Inc. official press release (PRNewswire) / CNBC / Broadcom IR
earnings.mostRecentQuarterNote: As of 2026-06-01, FQ1 2026 (reported 2026-03-04) is the MOST RECENT REPORTED quarter. FQ2 2026 (quarter ended ~May 3, 2026) is scheduled for release 2026-06-03 - two days after this run - so Q2 figures below are management GUIDANCE, not reported actuals. Broadcom's fiscal year ends in early November, so fiscal quarters lead calendar quarters by ~one month.

keyMetrics.aiRevenue: $8.4B, +106% YoY - custom AI accelerators (XPUs) and AI networking (component of Semiconductor Solutions, not a separate reported segment)
keyMetrics.softwareRevenue: $6.80B infrastructure software, +1% YoY (VMware revenue +13% YoY; VMware ARR +19%; TCV bookings $9.2B)

earnings.guidance: FORWARD GUIDANCE issued 2026-03-04 for the not-yet-reported FQ2 2026 (ending ~May 3, 2026): total revenue ~$22.0B, +47% YoY; adjusted EBITDA ~68% of revenue; FQ2 AI semiconductor revenue guided to $10.7B, ~140% YoY. Longer-term: CEO reiterated 'line of sight' to AI chip revenue in excess of $100B in 2027. Reported FQ2 actuals expected 2026-06-03.
earnings.aiCommentary: AI was the dominant theme. Q1 AI revenue of $8.4B grew 106% YoY (above Broadcom's own forecast), powered by custom AI accelerators (XPUs) and AI networking; the custom accelerator business grew ~140% YoY. Management highlighted strong hyperscaler/custom-silicon traction: Meta's MTIA roadmap is shipping now, and OpenAI is expected to deploy its first-generation XPU in volume in 2027 at over 1 gigawatt. CEO Hock Tan reiterated 'line of sight' to AI revenue from chips alone in excess of $100B in 2027. For FQ2 2026, management guided AI semiconductor revenue to accelerate to $10.7B, ~140% YoY.
earnings.demandCommentary: Total revenue of $19.3B (+29% YoY) was a record for the fiscal first quarter, with Semiconductor Solutions at $12.5B (+52% YoY) carried by AI demand; non-AI/core semiconductor demand was more muted. Infrastructure Software was $6.8B (+1% YoY, lapping the VMware integration); within software, VMware revenue grew 13% YoY, ARR up 19%, TCV bookings $9.2B. Management framed VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) as the software layer for AI infrastructure - arguing AI creates demand for 'more VMware, not less.' Capital returns: $10.9B returned in Q1 ($3.1B dividends + $7.8B buybacks); board authorized an additional $10.0B repurchase program.

keyQuotes.quote: Broadcom achieved record first quarter revenue on continued strength in AI semiconductor solutions. Q1 AI revenue of $8.4 billion grew 106% year-over-year, above our forecast, driven by robust demand for custom AI accelerators and AI networking.
keyQuotes.speaker: CEO Hock Tan

keyQuotes.quote: Today, in fact, we have line of sight to achieve AI revenue from chips, just chips, in excess of $100 billion in 2027.
keyQuotes.speaker: CEO Hock Tan
keyQuotes.source: Broadcom Q1 FY2026 earnings call transcript (The Globe and Mail / The Motley Fool)

keyQuotes.quote: In Q2, this momentum accelerates and we expect AI revenue growth to accelerate very sharply to 140% year on year to $10.7 billion.
keyQuotes.speaker: CEO Hock Tan
keyQuotes.source: Broadcom Q1 FY2026 earnings call transcript (The Globe and Mail / The Motley Fool)

keyQuotes.quote: In Q1, we paid stockholders $3.1 billion of cash dividends and repurchased $7.8 billion of stock... we returned $10.9 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.
keyQuotes.speaker: CFO Kirsten Spears
keyQuotes.source: Broadcom Q1 FY2026 earnings call transcript (The Globe and Mail / The Motley Fool)
